profiles.name: Gautier Queru
profiles.summary: Founded Mirova's natural capital practice in 2016 with the Land Degradation Neutrality Fund, co-promoted with the UN Convention to Combat Desertification. Has since raised over $1 billion for nature across two fund generations. Sub-Saharan Africa is a core geography for both the LDN Fund and its successor, Mirova Sustainable Land Fund 2, which targets regenerative agriculture and sustainable forestry across Africa, Latin America and Southeast Asia.

notable_work.description: Founded Mirova's natural capital investment practice and raised $208M for the LDN Fund, investing in ecosystem restoration and agroforestry across Africa and Latin America
Closed Mirova Sustainable Land Fund 2 above 100 million euros with DFI investors including FMO, Proparco, UK DEFRA and the Green Climate Fund

profiles.name: Magali Rousselot
profiles.summary: Investment Director within Mirova natural capital team, working alongside Gautier Queru on deal origination and portfolio management across Africa, Latin America and Southeast Asia. Prior to Mirova she held an investment role at Proparco, the private sector arm of Agence Francaise de Developpement, where she structured financing for projects in health and education across emerging markets. At Mirova she has been involved in individual transaction execution within the Sustainable Land Fund strategies.

notable_work.description: Lead investment director on the Bureo transaction, through which Mirova deployed capital into fishing net recycling and ocean plastics reduction across Latin America, contributing to Mirova passing $100 million in deployed impact capital

profiles.name: Irina Likhachova
profiles.role: Global Lead, Biodiversity and Nature Finance
profiles.summary: IFC's global lead on biodiversity and nature finance, responsible for the IFC Biodiversity Finance Reference Guide - the field's primary framework for investors seeking to identify eligible nature-positive investments. Leads IFC's engagement on nature-based solutions across emerging markets including Africa.

notable_work.description: Led the development of the IFC Biodiversity Finance Reference Guide, adopted by major investors and development banks as the standard for identifying and structuring nature-positive investments

profiles.name: Mary Porter Peschka
profiles.summary: IFC's senior relationship holder for Eastern Africa, overseeing the institution's private sector investment portfolio across Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda and the region. notable_work.description: IFC co-financing partner for Kenya's CIF Nature, People and Climate Investment Plan, focusing on nature-based tourism, reforestation and sustainable agriculture

profiles.name: Andrew Steer
profiles.summary: Leads the Bezos Earth Fund's $10 billion commitment to climate and nature. Announced $22.8 million in Africa landscape restoration grants at the 2023 Africa Climate Summit in Nairobi. The Fund's $200 million African biodiversity round, currently accepting applications for May 2026, is the largest philanthropic conservation commitment targeting Africa in the current cycle.

notable_work.description: Announced $22.8M for locally led restoration in the Greater Rift Valley and Lake Kivu/Rusizi Basin at the Africa Climate Summit 2023

Quoted alongside Andrew Steer at the Africa Climate Summit, she is the operational Africa lead for BEF's restoration and conservation programming. A prominent voice on locally led conservation across the continent.

notable_work.description: Led Africa programming for the Bezos Earth Fund's $22.8M landscape restoration grants, focusing on locally led restoration in Kenya and the DRC/Rwanda/Burundi basin

profiles.name: Mandy Rambharos
profiles.summary: South African climate negotiator who led Africa's delegation at the UNFCCC for over 15 years and co-chaired the Article 6 outcome at COP26 - the first operational carbon market rulebook under the Paris Agreement. Appointed CEO of Verra in 2024, making her the most senior African leader in the global voluntary carbon market. Previously ran just energy transition at Eskom.

notable_work.description: Co-chaired the Article 6 negotiations at COP26 in Glasgow, producing the first agreed rules for international carbon trading under the Paris Agreement
Appointed CEO of Verra, the world's largest voluntary carbon standard, overseeing over 2,000 registered projects globally

profiles.name: Marc Baker
profiles.summary: Co-Founder and Director of Carbon Tanzania, one of Africa most respected community-centred forest carbon project developers, which he co-founded in 2007. Carbon Tanzania works with indigenous communities in northern and western Tanzania to protect dryland forests and generate Verra-verified carbon credits, with all revenues flowing directly to the communities. Projects now cover more than 690,000 hectares of forest across four REDD schemes, protecting habitat for chimpanzees, elephants, wild dogs and lions, and have delivered more than $3.7 million directly to remote communities in 2024 alone. BSc in Conservation Management from Nottingham Trent University. Based in Arusha.

notable_work.description: Co-founded Carbon Tanzania in 2007, validating the first REDD project in 2011 and growing to four operational forest conservation projects covering over 690,000 hectares in Tanzania, with over $6.9 million delivered to communities in 2023

profiles.name: David Antonioli
profiles.summary: Founding CEO of Verra, the world leading carbon credit standards body, serving from its inception until May 2023. Antonioli built Verra into the dominant standard in the voluntary carbon market, overseeing the development of the Verified Carbon Standard and its growth to over one billion credits issued. He departed in May 2023 following criticism of credit quality and a significant revenue decline. Succeeded by interim CEO Judith Simon, then by current CEO Mandy Rambharos from August 2024. Antonioli subsequently moved into advisory and investment roles in the carbon markets space.

notable_work.description: Led Verra through the approval of VM0047 ARR methodology, now recognised by ICVCM as meeting Core Carbon Principles and the cornerstone of high-integrity forestry credits in Africa

profiles.name: Eron Bloomgarden
profiles.summary: Founder and Executive Director of Emergent, which manages the LEAF Coalition - a public-private initiative that has mobilised over $1.5 billion in commitments for jurisdictional-scale REDD+ programmes. Several African nations are active LEAF participants. Emergent sits at the intersection of sovereign carbon ambition and corporate buyer demand, making Bloomgarden one of the most influential architects of large-scale tropical forest finance.

notable_work.description: Founded the LEAF Coalition in partnership with the US, UK and Norwegian governments, mobilising corporate commitments for high-integrity tropical forest carbon from eligible jurisdictions including Ghana and Cote d Ivoire

profiles.name: Alice Ruhweza
profiles.summary: WWF Africa Regional Director, leading the organisation across 22 African offices. A recognised voice on biodiversity finance, community conservation, and the intersection of conservation and development in Africa. Ugandan. Sits on advisory boards including the Global Environment Facility and contributes to African positions in global conservation finance negotiations.

profiles.name: Will Rayment
profiles.summary: Co-CEO of Carbon Tanzania, one of East Africa's most established community-based REDD+ developers. Projects include the Yaeda Valley project with the Hadzabe hunter-gatherers - one of the most widely cited examples of community-owned carbon finance in Africa. Carbon Tanzania's model prioritises land tenure and direct community benefit over third-party intermediation.

notable_work.description: Developed the Yaeda Valley REDD+ project with the Hadzabe community in Tanzania, distributing carbon revenues directly to one of Africa oldest hunter-gatherer groups

profiles.name: Charlotte Kaiser
profiles.summary: Head of Impact Finance at BTG Pactual Timberland Investment Group, the first person to hold this role at one of the world largest timberland investment managers with $7.5 billion in assets. Previously Managing Director of NatureVest at The Nature Conservancy, TNC impact investment team, where she grew the portfolio to more than $2.5 billion committed to conservation transactions. Started her career at TNC developing the Conservation Notes, which raised $25 million from impact investors. BA from Harvard in Environmental Science and Public Policy, Masters in Environmental Science and MBA from Yale.

notable_work.description: Led NatureVest at TNC, growing the impact investment portfolio to more than $2.5 billion committed to conservation transactions that deliver both financial returns and conservation outcomes

profiles.name: Dilys Roe
profiles.organisation: International Institute for Environment and Development
profiles.summary: Lead researcher at IIED on biodiversity finance and the governance of conservation at the community level. Work spans thirty years across East, West and Southern Africa examining how finance reaches communities with legitimate land claims and what conditions determine whether it improves or undermines livelihoods. One of the sector most rigorous voices on additionality and benefit-sharing design.

profiles.name: Gilles Kleitz
profiles.role: Deputy Associate Director for Science, Sustainability Sciences
profiles.organisation: Institut de Recherche pour le Developpement (IRD)
profiles.summary: Deputy Associate Director for Science in charge of Sustainability Sciences at the Institut de Recherche pour le Developpement (IRD), appointed June 2024. Previously spent six years at AFD (Agence Francaise de Developpement) as director of the ecological and natural transition department and then Executive Director for Sustainable Development Solutions (2022-2024), during which he committed AFD to doubling biodiversity finance to €1 billion annually and aligning 30 percent of climate finance with biodiversity outcomes. Career spanning over 30 years in conservation and sustainable development across more than 40 countries. Director of Parc Amazonien de Guyane (2014-2018). Knight of the Legion of Honour. Agronomist, general engineer of bridges, water and forests, PhD in political science from Sussex.

notable_work.description: Drove AFD commitment to €1 billion annually in biodiversity finance and 30 percent alignment of climate finance with biodiversity outcomes, making AFD one of the first development banks to jointly address climate and nature

profiles.name: Liesel van Ast
profiles.summary: Deputy Head of the UN Environment Programme Finance Initiative (UNEP FI), the global partnership that brings together UNEP with more than 500 banks, insurers and investors to develop the sustainable finance agenda. Oversees financial sector engagement, regional coordination and capacity building for financial institutions on global sustainability frameworks and the UN-convened net-zero alliances for banks, insurers and asset owners. Joined UNEP FI in 2016 as Signatory Relations Manager, previously Programme Manager of the Natural Capital Declaration at Global Canopy Programme and UNEP FI, and before that at Trucost and The ENDS Report. Member of the TNFD Stewardship Council.

notable_work.description: Leads UNEP FI engagement on the Principles for Responsible Banking, Principles for Sustainable Insurance and net-zero alliances, coordinating sustainable finance capacity building across 500 member institutions globally

profiles.name: Henry Neufeldt
profiles.summary: Research Director for Climate Change at CIFOR-ICRAF (Center for International Forestry Research and World Agroforestry), based in Nairobi. Leads research on climate change mitigation and adaptation in the context of agroforestry, forests and land use, with a focus on climate-smart agriculture, bioenergy, and greenhouse gas fluxes. Previously Head of the Climate Change Unit at the World Agroforestry Centre (ICRAF) and ICRAF focal point for the CGIAR Research Program on Climate Change, Agriculture and Food Security. Has been an advisor on AFOLU climate bonds to the Climate Bonds Initiative. PhD from Bayreuth University in environmental sciences and soil science.

profiles.name: Jan Cassin
profiles.summary: Senior Advisor for the Water Initiative at Forest Trends, previously Director of the Water Initiative where she led the organisation work on scaling nature-based solutions for sustainable water management. Has worked extensively on investments in watershed services, green infrastructure, and payment for ecosystem services frameworks globally. PhD from the University of Michigan. Former biologist at The Nature Conservancy and water resources consultant. Author of the book Nature-Based Solutions and Water Security (2021).

notable_work.description: Led Forest Trends Water Initiative work on scaling nature-based solutions for water security globally, including co-authoring the landmark book 'Nature-Based Solutions and Water Security' (Elsevier, 2021)

profiles.name: Eve Bazaiba
profiles.role: Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Environment
profiles.organisation: Government of the Democratic Republic of Congo
profiles.summary: Former Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of the Environment and Sustainable Development of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2021-August 2025). A lawyer, politician and human rights activist, she was one of the most prominent African voices on forests and nature finance during her tenure. Chaired the CAFI partnership for forest conservation in the Congo Basin and represented DRC in major climate negotiations. Handed over the ministry to Marie Niange Ndambo in August 2025. Born 1965 in Kisangani.
profiles._note: Eve Bazaiba left the Environment ministry in August 2025 and was replaced by Marie Niange Ndambo. The new DRC government formed in 2024 listed her as Minister of State for Environment (not Deputy PM). Entry is outdated. Update role to reflect former status or replace with current minister.

profiles.name: Vikram Widge
profiles.summary: Senior Advisor at Climate Policy Initiative (CPI), where he supports strategy and programmatic development on climate finance. Previously the Global Head of Climate Finance and Policy at IFC, the private sector arm of the World Bank Group, for more than 30 years. At IFC, he built the carbon finance business from scratch (reaching $250 million under management), launched the inaugural $152 million Forests Bond (Environmental Finance 2017 Deal of the Year), and led the first TCFD-aligned reporting by a multilateral development bank. Also a Senior Fellow at the Atlantic Council Arsht-Rockefeller Foundation Resilience Center and on the advisory boards of GoImpact Capital Partners and Arara.io. IETA Honorary Fellow. IIT Delhi engineering graduate with a masters in resource economics from the University of Maryland.

notable_work.description: Led the inaugural Forests Bond at IFC - a $152 million instrument that paid investors based on verified emissions reductions from avoided deforestation, recognised as the 2017 Sustainable Forestry Deal of the Year by Environmental Finance

profiles.name: Rob Dodsworth
profiles.summary: Vice President for Conservation Finance at AWF, one of Africa oldest and most operationally embedded conservation NGOs. Leads AWF effort to structure investment-grade conservation transactions that blend philanthropic, public, and private capital to protect priority landscapes across East and Southern Africa. AWF manages direct partnerships with dozens of African governments and communities.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Rob Dodsworth as Vice President Conservation Finance at AWF. AWF leadership page does not confirm this role. Do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: Peter Munya
profiles.role: Cabinet Secretary, Environment, Climate Change and Forestry
profiles.summary: Kenya Cabinet Secretary responsible for environment and climate, overseeing the country Article 6.2 bilateral framework and its engagement with Switzerland, Singapore, and other ITMO buyer countries. Kenya is one of East Africa most active Article 6.2 countries and the minister is the political principal behind its carbon diplomacy strategy.
profiles._note: Peter Munya was Cabinet Secretary for Agriculture, Livestock, Fisheries and Cooperatives - NOT for Environment, Climate Change and Forestry. The entry has the wrong ministerial role. The CS for Environment has been Soipan Tuya (2022-2024), then Aden Duale briefly, then Deborah Barasa from March 2025. Do not publish this entry without verification of correct role or replacement with the correct person.

profiles.name: Esperance Mukamana
profiles.summary: Leads REMA carbon markets programme, overseeing Rwanda engagement with Article 6.2 bilateral partners and the development of the country national carbon framework. Rwanda has been among the more methodical African governments in building its Article 6.2 governance architecture and REMA is the technical lead on all carbon market authorisation decisions.
profiles._note: Unverified. Esperance Mukamana as Head of Carbon Markets Programme at Rwanda Environment Management Authority (REMA) - needs current role confirmation.

profiles.name: Lee Ann Tronc
profiles.summary: Partner in the finance practice at Latham and Watkins, one of the law firms most active in structuring debt-for-nature swap transactions in Africa. Debt-for-nature deals are legally complex sovereign transactions requiring specialist counsel who can navigate simultaneous requirements of creditor agreements, conservation covenants, trust fund structures, and national legislation. The transaction lawyers are invisible but indispensable.
profiles._note: Unverified. Lee Ann Tronc as Partner at Latham and Watkins - needs current role confirmation in conservation/carbon market legal work.

profiles.name: Yolande Wright
profiles.role: Director, Climate Finance and Carbon Markets
profiles.summary: Leads WCS engagement on carbon markets and conservation finance across its Africa programmes. WCS operates in some of the continents most complex conservation landscapes including the Congo Basin, South Sudan, and Madagascar, and has been developing carbon finance as a mechanism to sustain field operations in areas where grant funding alone cannot cover programme costs.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Yolande Wright as Director, Climate Finance and Carbon Markets at WCS. WCS staff pages and leadership pages do not confirm this specific role. Do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: Guy Pinjuv
profiles.summary: Managing Director for Africa at Great Plains Conservation, a Botswana-based conservation company that operates a portfolio of high-end safari camps and conservation concessions across Botswana, Kenya, Zimbabwe and Tanzania. Great Plains has been developing biodiversity credits and carbon programmes across its managed landscapes, positioning premium tourism as the base revenue layer for a broader conservation finance stack.
profiles._note: Unverified. Guy Pinjuv as Managing Director Africa at Great Plains Conservation - needs current role confirmation.

profiles.name: Jochen Krimphoff
profiles.role: Global Lead, Data, Tools and Methodologies
profiles.summary: Global Lead for Data, Tools and Methodologies at WWF Greening Financial Regulation Initiative (GFRI), which engages with central banks, financial supervisors and regulators globally on integrating climate and environmental risks into financial decision-making. Based primarily in Paris. Has extensive experience advising policymakers on sustainable finance and served as a member of several European Commission expert groups. From 2017 to 2022 collaborated with international standard-setting organisations on global market standards for green debt capital markets. Previously at WWF-France as Deputy Director for Environmental Finance. Formerly at PricewaterhouseCoopers specialising in sustainable finance.

profiles.name: Stephen Donofrio
profiles.summary: Managing Director of Ecosystem Marketplace at Forest Trends, the leading data and intelligence platform for voluntary carbon markets. Responsible for the annual State of the Voluntary Carbon Markets report, the most widely cited data series on VCM transaction volumes and pricing globally. Previously Director of Supply Change at Forest Trends, and before that Vice President at CDP North America and Economist at the Chicago Climate Exchange. Founder of GreenPoint Innovations, a sustainability communications and arts company. Also serves as Educator with Boston College Center for Corporate Citizenship.

notable_work.description: Leads publication of the State of the Voluntary Carbon Markets annual report at Ecosystem Marketplace - the primary global data source on VCM transaction volumes, prices, and project types

profiles.name: Charlotte Streck
profiles.summary: Co-founder and director of Climate Focus, one of the most influential advisory firms in carbon market policy and design. Streck has been central to the architecture of Article 6.2 rule-making and has advised multiple African governments on their bilateral ITMO frameworks. Her work sits at the intersection of international climate law and practical transaction design.

profiles.name: Tosi Mpanu Mpanu
profiles.organisation: Government of the Democratic Republic of Congo
profiles.summary: Lead Climate Negotiator for the Democratic Republic of Congo and one of Africa most experienced UNFCCC negotiators. Former chair of the Least Developed Countries Expert Group and a veteran of Paris Agreement negotiations. Has been the DRC lead voice at climate COPs including on Article 6 carbon market mechanisms, REDD+ and forest finance. Based in Kinshasa.
profiles._note: Role and prominence confirmed from UNFCCC records. Verify current negotiating mandate.

profiles.name: Ibrahim Thiaw
profiles.summary: Executive Secretary of the UNCCD and one of Africa most senior voices in global environmental governance. Senegalese diplomat and former UNEP Deputy Executive Director. The UNCCD Land Degradation Neutrality framework is the policy spine behind the LDN Fund and several of the largest blended finance vehicles targeting African land restoration. Thiaw has made African land rights and dryland restoration central to his tenure.

profiles.name: Giulia Carbone
profiles.summary: Director of the Natural Climate Solutions Alliance at the World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD), a platform co-supported with the World Economic Forum that convenes public and private actors to drive demand for high-quality natural climate solutions carbon credits. Previously at IUCN for 15 years, latterly as Deputy Director of the Global Business and Biodiversity Programme. Before IUCN, spent eight years at UNEP. Also a member of the ICVCM. MSc in marine policy from LSE and masters in environmental engineering from Turin Polytechnic.

notable_work.description: Launched and leads the Natural Climate Solutions Alliance at WBCSD since October 2021, developing tools and guidelines for corporate procurement of high-quality NCS carbon credits and convening major private sector actors around nature-based carbon markets

profiles.name: Sebastien Rispal
profiles.summary: Managing Director of the Livelihoods Carbon Fund, a corporate-funded vehicle that finances large-scale agroforestry and mangrove restoration projects across Africa, Asia and Latin America. The Fund is backed by major European corporates including Danone, Schneider Electric and Mars, and has financed projects covering several million hectares across Senegal, Kenya, Madagascar and the Sahel.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Sebastien Rispal as Managing Director of Livelihoods Carbon Fund. Livelihoods team page shows CEO as Eric Soubeiran and President as Bernard Giraud. No Sebastien Rispal found in Livelihoods leadership. Entry may be incorrect - do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: Derik Broekhoff
profiles.summary: Senior Scientist at SEI and one of the most technically rigorous researchers working on voluntary carbon market quality and additionality. His published work on over-crediting, baseline manipulation, and permanence risks in forest carbon projects is widely cited by standard bodies, regulators, and institutional buyers. His assessments of African carbon credit quality carry significant weight with compliance-grade buyers.

profiles.name: Damilola Ogunbiyi
profiles.role: CEO and Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General
profiles.summary: CEO of Sustainable Energy for All and UN Special Representative on Sustainable Energy. Nigerian. A prominent convener of public and private capital for African infrastructure and climate transition, with deep relationships across African governments, multilateral institutions, and corporate energy buyers. SE4All co-manages several blended finance facilities relevant to nature-adjacent transition assets in Africa.

profiles.name: Sven Braden
profiles.summary: Executive Director of Convergence, the global network for blended finance that tracks deal flow and publishes the most comprehensive annual data on blended finance structures globally. Africa is the largest recipient geography in Convergence data. The organisation also runs design funding windows that subsidise the cost of structuring first-of-kind blended conservation finance transactions.
profiles._note: Cannot verify this person as Executive Director at Convergence Blended Finance. Convergence CEO is Joan M. Larrea. The real Sven Braden found online is a project manager at Wood Tracking Protocol. This entry appears to be incorrect - do not publish.

profiles.name: Paula Caballero
profiles.summary: Regional Managing Director for Latin America at The Nature Conservancy. Widely credited as the lead architect of the Sustainable Development Goals, which she conceptualized and negotiated while serving as Director for Economic, Social and Environmental Affairs in Colombia Ministry of Foreign Affairs (2010-2014). Received the Zayed International Prize for the Environment in 2014 and the German Sustainability Award in 2019. Previously Senior Director at the World Bank (2014-2016) and Global Director for Climate at the World Resources Institute (2016-), where she led the establishment of the NDC Partnership. Colombian national with a Masters in International Relations from Pontificia Universidad Javeriana.

notable_work.description: Conceptualized and negotiated the Sustainable Development Goals as Colombia Director for Economic, Social and Environmental Affairs - widely credited as the original architect of the SDG framework

profiles.name: Sara Scherr
profiles.summary: Founder of EcoAgriculture Partners, the Washington DC-based non-profit she led as President and CEO for more than two decades before stepping down in early 2025. Also stepped down as co-chair of the 1000 Landscapes for 1 Billion People steering committee at the same time, remaining on the EcoAgriculture board. Agricultural and natural resource economist specialising in integrated landscape management in tropical developing countries. Founded EcoAgriculture Partners in 2002. PhD and MSc in International Economics and Development from Cornell University. Former Director of Ecosystem Services at Forest Trends, senior researcher at the International Food Policy Research Institute, and co-leader of the CGIAR Gender Program.

notable_work.description: Founded EcoAgriculture Partners in 2002 and led it for over 20 years, establishing the integrated landscape management (ILM) field and building the 1000 Landscapes for 1 Billion People initiative into a 40-partner coalition across six continents

profiles.name: Ben Goldsmith
profiles.summary: CEO of Menhaden Capital, a London-listed investment trust focused on energy and resource efficiency, founded in 2015. Also chairs Conservation Collective, a philanthropic network supporting 21 locally-based environmental foundations from Devon to Sri Lanka, and chairs the Conservative Environment Network. A trustee of the Children Investment Fund Foundation (CIFF), one of the world largest climate-focused philanthropic funds. Previously co-founded WHEB Asset Management in 2003, now one of Europe leading sustainability-focused fund management businesses. Non-Executive Director at DEFRA from 2018 to 2022. Author of God Is An Octopus: Loss, Love and A Calling to Nature (Bloomsbury, 2023). Son of James Goldsmith; attended Eton.

notable_work.description: Co-founded WHEB Asset Management in 2003, one of the UK early specialist sustainability investment businesses, before launching Menhaden Capital in 2015 as a London-listed thematic trust focused on energy and resource efficiency

profiles.name: John Godfrey
profiles.summary: Leads Acorn Carbon, Rabobank Group smallholder tree-planting and agroforestry carbon programme. Acorn aggregates tiny plots from tens of thousands of African smallholder farmers into tradeable carbon credits, solving the aggregation problem that has historically prevented smallholder-scale projects from accessing the voluntary carbon market. Kenya is the primary geography.
profiles._note: Unverified. John Godfrey as Director at Acorn Carbon - needs current role confirmation.

profiles.name: James Mayers
profiles.organisation: International Institute for Environment and Development
profiles.summary: Principal researcher at IIED on forest governance, tenure, and the political economy of conservation in Africa. His work on who controls forest land - and therefore who benefits from forest carbon - is foundational to understanding why so many African REDD+ projects have failed to deliver community benefit. Operates across Central and West Africa including DRC, Cameroon, and Ghana.

profiles.name: Florence Landsberg
profiles.summary: Director at WRI leading its wildlife and biodiversity conservation work, including research on the financing mechanisms that underpin protected area management across Africa. WRI Global Forest Watch platform is a primary data source for REDD+ project monitoring and African deforestation intelligence.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Florence Landsberg as Director, Wildlife Conservation Initiative at WRI. WRI profile shows her as a Research Associate with the Global Restoration Initiative team - a much more junior role. The Wildlife Conservation Initiative is a separate NAFO/USFWS initiative unrelated to WRI. Entry likely has wrong title and/or role description. Do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: Yemi Katerere
profiles.summary: Coordinator of the African CSO Biodiversity Alliance (ACBA), a network of African civil society organisations focused on biodiversity and climate change. Independent environmental expert with more than three decades of experience in forest research, biodiversity policy, REDD+, and sustainable development across Africa. Previously held roles at IUCN, WWF Africa, and the UN. Recipient of the Queen Award for outstanding contribution to forestry development in Zimbabwe. Has chaired the World Agroforestry Board, served as non-executive director of the Centre for International Forestry Research (CIFOR) and UN-REDD Programme board. PhD-level researcher with work across miombo woodlands, REDD+, and ecosystem services.

profiles.name: Kelley Hamrick
profiles.role: Senior Policy Advisor, International Climate Policy
profiles.summary: Senior Policy Advisor for International Climate Policy at The Nature Conservancy, working on the intersection of policy and voluntary carbon markets and the Paris Agreement. Previously spent a decade at Ecosystem Marketplace (Forest Trends) as Programme Manager, where she was the lead author of the flagship State of the Voluntary Carbon Markets report across multiple years and a leading analyst of the carbon markets and conservation finance fields. Won the Climate Action Reserve CARROT Award in 2019 for contributions to the carbon markets field.

notable_work.description: Lead author of the State of the Voluntary Carbon Markets report at Ecosystem Marketplace across multiple years, the flagship annual data publication on voluntary carbon market transaction volumes, prices and project types globally

profiles.name: Bernd Schanzenbacher
profiles.summary: Founder and Managing Partner of EBG Capital, a specialist agricultural investment advisory firm. Co-founded responsAbility Investments in 2003, one of the pioneers of impact investing in emerging markets and now one of the world largest such platforms with $5.5 billion AUM. Has since moved to an independent advisory role, guiding family offices and institutional investors in agricultural and timberland investments globally including across Africa. Author of papers on farmland as an asset class and responsible governance of tenure. Over 25 years experience in agricultural and sustainability investment.

notable_work.description: Co-founded responsAbility Investments in 2003, building it into one of the world leading impact asset managers with over USD 16.8 billion deployed in emerging markets since inception

profiles.name: Michael Wolosin
profiles.summary: Managing Director at Climate Advisers, a Washington strategy firm that advises governments and major NGOs on forest carbon policy. Has worked on tropical forest finance strategy for over a decade and advises African governments on REDD+ positioning and Article 6.2 negotiating strategy. His analysis of supply-demand dynamics in forest carbon is frequently cited by institutional buyers.
profiles._note: Unverified. Michael Wolosin as Managing Director at Climate Advisers - needs current role confirmation.

profiles.name: Mpho Tshivhase
profiles.summary: Leads climate finance at DBSA, the South African development finance institution with a mandate across Southern Africa. DBSA is a co-financier on several of South Africa major climate and conservation programmes and is central to structuring blended finance vehicles that meet South African government requirements. Its positioning on carbon markets and Article 6.2 matters for the Southern African region.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Mpho Tshivhase as Head of Climate Finance at DBSA. DBSA executive team page does not list this person. Do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: Pedro Barata
profiles.role: Associate Vice President, Carbon Markets and Private Sector Decarbonization
profiles.summary: Associate Vice President for Carbon Markets and Private Sector Decarbonization at Environmental Defense Fund. Leads EDF work on voluntary and compliance carbon market integrity including the Carbon Credit Quality Initiative and co-chairs the Expert Panel of the Integrity Council for the Voluntary Carbon Market (ICVCM). Portuguese national with more than 20 years in international climate policy and carbon markets - negotiated under the Kyoto Protocol from 1999 and led Portugal negotiations on the EU Emissions Trading System. Former vice chair of the Clean Development Mechanism at UNFCCC. Has assisted governments in Colombia, Mexico, Costa Rica and Morocco on domestic carbon pricing. MSc in Environmental Policy from the London School of Economics.

notable_work.description: Co-Chair of the Expert Panel of the Integrity Council for the Voluntary Carbon Market (ICVCM), which sets global threshold standards for high-integrity carbon credits including the Core Carbon Principles

profiles.name: Achim Steiner
profiles.summary: Administrator of UNDP and former Executive Director of UNEP. German-Brazilian. One of the most senior environmental officials in the UN system and the institutional principal behind UNDP major Africa biodiversity and climate finance programmes. His decade at UNEP before UNDP included the launch of REDD+ and the early architecture of environmental markets.

profiles.name: Dr. Tom Crowther
profiles.summary: Professor at ETH Zurich and founder of the Crowther Lab, whose 2019 Science paper on global tree restoration potential is the most cited piece of science in the afforestation and reforestation carbon market. His subsequent research on which areas are ecologically appropriate for tree planting has become essential reading for ARR methodology developers and is directly relevant to VM0047 project design in Africa.

notable_work.description: Led the publication of the global tree restoration potential study in Science, setting off a decade of debate about the scale and limits of afforestation as a carbon drawdown strategy

profiles.name: Naoko Ishii
profiles.organisation: Center for Global Commons, University of Tokyo
profiles.summary: Director of the Center for Global Commons at the University of Tokyo and former CEO of the Global Environment Facility. Japanese. Her decade leading the GEF gave her direct experience disbursing billions in biodiversity and climate finance across Africa. Now works on systemic approaches to planetary boundaries and the governance architecture for global commons finance.

profiles.name: Penelope Twemlow
profiles.summary: Leads conservation finance at Fauna and Flora International, one of the oldest conservation NGOs in the world with field programmes across East Africa and the Indian Ocean islands. FFI has been developing carbon finance as a complement to its field operations and is active in structuring community benefit-sharing agreements that meet both conservation and carbon standard requirements.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Penelope Twemlow as Director Conservation Finance at Fauna and Flora International. FFI staff page does not confirm this role. Do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: Laetitia Pettinotti
profiles.summary: Research fellow at ODI working on climate finance flows to developing countries, with a focus on Africa. Tracks and analyses the gap between climate finance commitments and actual disbursement to African nations, and has published assessments of how conservation finance instruments reach or fail to reach least developed countries. Her data on delivery gaps is frequently cited in policy discussions.

profiles.name: Stephen Kelleher
profiles.summary: Director at ACMI working on mobilising institutional capital into African carbon markets. ACMI was launched at COP27 in Sharm el-Sheikh with the goal of generating 300 million carbon credits annually across Africa by 2030 and channelling $6 billion to the continent. Kelleher works on the supply-side transaction pipeline and investor engagement strategy.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Stephen Kelleher as Director of Capital Mobilisation at ACMI. ACMI leadership is not publicly detailed. Do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: Wanjiku Kamau
profiles.summary: Senior official in the Kenya Ministry of Environment responsible for the country carbon markets programme, including the Article 6.2 bilateral arrangement pipeline and the national framework governing carbon credit authorisation. Kenya has one of the most active Article 6.2 pipelines in Africa and the ministry is the technical decision-making body on ITMO eligibility and transfer authorisation.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Wanjiku Kamau as Head of Carbon Markets at Kenya Ministry of Environment. Kenya government carbon markets function not confirmed for this person. Do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: Nerea Calvillo
profiles.summary: Director of conservation finance at WCS, leading the organisation strategy for integrating carbon and biodiversity markets into its field programme funding model. WCS operates across some of the most biodiversity-rich and financially underserved landscapes in Africa including the Congo Basin, and is developing REDD+ and biodiversity credit pipelines in DRC, Gabon, and the Central African Republic.
profiles._note: Cannot verify Nerea Calvillo as Director, Conservation Finance at WCS. WCS staff pages and leadership pages do not confirm this specific role. Do not publish without verification.

profiles.name: David Brand
profiles.summary: Founder of New Forests, one of the world leading institutional investment managers in forestry and natural capital, which he built over 17 years before stepping down as CEO in April 2023. New Forests manages over AUD 9.95 billion in assets across more than 1.1 million hectares globally. Before New Forests he led investment programs at Hancock Natural Resource Group (2000-2005) and before that was Executive General Manager of State Forests of NSW and national Director-General of Science and Sustainable Development for the Canadian federal Department of Forests. PhD from the University of British Columbia. Board member of Forest Trends and the Centre for People and Forests.

notable_work.description: Founded New Forests in 2005 and built it into one of the world leading institutional forestry and natural capital fund managers over 17 years, with AUD 9.95 billion in assets under management at the time of stepping down as CEO

profiles.name: Duko Hopman
profiles.summary: Executive Director of the Rob Walton Foundation - the first person to hold this role. RWF is the primary funder behind the Africa Keystone Protected Area Partnership, which it co-founded with African Parks, WCS, and the Frankfurt Zoological Society and launched at UNGA in September 2025. Hopman brings deep experience from McKinsey where he led global natural capital and conservation finance initiatives, with Africa as his primary geography. RWF made a $100 million five-year commitment to African Parks in 2021 and funds Conservation International, the Blue Nature Alliance, and the Legacy Landscapes Fund, among other grantees.

notable_work.description: Appointed as first Executive Director of the Rob Walton Foundation to lead its Africa-focused conservation strategy
Oversaw the Africa Keystone Protected Area Partnership launch at UNGA September 2025, with 20 African governments and four founding NGO partners

public_footprint.title: Welcoming Duko Hopman - Rob Walton Foundation

profiles.name: Daniela Raik
profiles.summary: Led Conservation International as Interim CEO from August 2025 to February 2026, following the departure of M. Sanjayan. Previously served as Executive Vice President and Chief Conservation Officer, leading CI conservation impact across nearly 30 countries. Has worked at CI for 17 years across roles including managing director of the Betty and Gordon Moore Center for Science and senior vice president of the Americas program. Holds advanced degrees in natural resources from Cornell University. Was instrumental in developing the Gaborone Declaration for Sustainability in Africa. Succeeded as permanent CEO by Dr Sebastian Troeng in February 2026. CI is a primary grantee of the Rob Walton Foundation and a founding partner in the Blue Nature Alliance.

notable_work.description: Appointed Interim CEO of Conservation International following departure of M. Sanjayan, leading the organisation through a period of strategic transition

profiles.name: Stefanie Lang
profiles.summary: Executive Director of the Legacy Landscapes Fund since April 2021, leading the Frankfurt-based charitable foundation that provides long-term secured core funding for globally significant protected areas. LLF was established by the German Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development and KfW Development Bank, and targets a portfolio of 30 landscapes by 2030 across Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Lang brings more than 20 years of conservation experience across bilateral and multilateral institutions, NGOs, and the private sector. LLF African portfolio includes Odzala-Kokoua (Republic of Congo), Iona (Angola), Gonarezhou (Zimbabwe), and other Keystone Protected Areas. LLF is a primary grantee of the Rob Walton Foundation.

notable_work.description: Built the Legacy Landscapes Fund portfolio to 15 landscapes across Africa, Asia, and Latin America, presenting at a Berlin portfolio exhibition in October 2025 with 15 partner landscapes

profiles.name: Ryan Dolan
profiles.summary: Managing Director of the Blue Nature Alliance, a global marine conservation partnership co-led by Conservation International, The Pew Charitable Trusts, the Global Environment Facility, Minderoo Foundation, and the Rob and Melani Walton Foundation. The Alliance targets the conservation of 18 million square kilometres of ocean by 2030. Dolan brings more than eight years of experience with The Pew Charitable Trusts working to secure marine protected areas in Antarctica Southern Ocean, including serving as NGO representative to the US delegation at CCAMLR and playing a central role in securing the Ross Sea Protected Area in 2016. Blue Nature Alliance is a primary vehicle for RWF ocean conservation strategy and a key grantee in the RWF portfolio alongside African Parks, CI, and the Legacy Landscapes Fund.

notable_work.description: Leads the Blue Nature Alliance in coordinating monitoring, annual planning, and Antarctic site engagement across the 18-million-km2 ocean conservation coalition

profiles.name: Rob Walton
profiles.summary: Founder and Chairman of the Rob Walton Foundation, established to promote sustainable development and responsible natural resource stewardship with Africa as its primary conservation geography. Former Chairman of Walmart. One of the most significant new entrants in African conservation philanthropy, focused on safeguarding critical landscapes vital to the livelihoods and security of millions of people.

profiles.name: Ben Walton
profiles.summary: Board Director of the Rob Walton Foundation, serving on the governing body responsible for strategic oversight, fiduciary accountability, and alignment with the Foundation vision for conservation-focused investment in Africa.

profiles.name: Rachel Penner
profiles.summary: Board Director of the Rob Walton Foundation, serving on the governing body responsible for strategic oversight and fiduciary accountability for its Africa-focused conservation portfolio.

profiles.name: Peter Seligmann
profiles.summary: Board Director of the Rob Walton Foundation and co-founder of Conservation International, which he led for over thirty years and built into one of the world largest conservation organisations with deep Africa field programmes. Seligmann was among the earliest champions of market-based conservation finance and brings decades of conservation deal-making experience to the Foundation strategic direction.

notable_work.description: Co-founded Conservation International in 1987 and led it for over thirty years, building a global institution with major biodiversity programmes across Africa

profiles.name: Barry Gold
profiles.summary: Senior conservation advisor to the Rob Walton Foundation. Described by the Foundation as a conservation leader whose guidance shapes its strategic approach to Africa. Continues in an advisory capacity alongside the Foundation first Executive Director.

public_footprint.title: Welcoming Duko Hopman - Rob Walton Foundation

profiles.name: Tony Hansen
profiles.role: Senior Advisor, Natural Capital and Nature
profiles.summary: Senior advisor on natural capital and nature with over thirty years of experience across conservation, sustainability, and high-impact programmes. Former Director of McKinsey Natural Capital and Nature group - where he helped companies on the journey to nature-positive and assisted governments in designing and implementing the UN Global Biodiversity Framework. University of Cape Town graduate with deep Southern Africa roots. Advisory board member of Tompkins Conservation. Co-created a transformative sustainable agriculture partnership between the South African wine industry and the conservation sector earlier in his career.

notable_work.description: Directed McKinsey Natural Capital and Nature group, advising multinationals and governments on nature-positive strategy and implementation of the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework

profiles.name: Peter Baldwin
profiles.summary: Co-founder of Arcadia, the charitable fund he established with his wife Lisbet Rausing in 2001 and which has awarded more than $1.3 billion to conservation, cultural heritage and open access organisations globally. A research professor of history at UCLA and Global Distinguished Professor at NYU - his academic work on comparative European and American history runs alongside his philanthropy. Arcadia has been a major funder of conservation in Africa through its support for the Halcyon Land and Sea programme with Fauna and Flora International, which has brought over 9.6 million hectares under conservation management. Also chairs the board of the Center for Jewish History and sits on the boards of the New York Public Library and the American Council of Learned Societies.

notable_work.description: Co-founded Arcadia in 2001 with Lisbet Rausing and has awarded more than $1.3 billion in grants for nature conservation, endangered cultural heritage, and open access to knowledge, including landmark support for Fauna and Flora International Halcyon Land and Sea programme

profiles.name: Antha Williams
profiles.summary: Head of the Environment Program at Bloomberg Philanthropies, which supports Michael Bloomberg personal efforts on climate including his role as UN Secretary-General Special Envoy on Climate Ambition and Solutions. Directs grants and initiatives covering the coal-to-clean energy transition, city climate action, ocean protection, air quality, and sustainable finance. Sits on the boards of C40 Cities, ClimateWorks Foundation, European Climate Foundation, Climate Imperative, League of Conservation Voters, and Oceana. Previously Senior Vice President at Corridor Partners, a donor advisory firm focused on climate and clean energy, and Advocacy Executive at Atlantic Philanthropies. Dartmouth College graduate.

notable_work.description: Leads Bloomberg Philanthropies environment programme across coal phase-out, city climate action and ocean protection, distributing $3.7 billion in Bloomberg Philanthropies grants in 2024 and overseeing the Vibrant Oceans Initiative and Beyond Carbon campaign

profiles.name: Brian O'Donnell
profiles.summary: Director of Campaign for Nature, the Wyss Foundation-backed initiative that drove the global 30x30 commitment - the pledge to protect 30 percent of land and ocean by 2030 now adopted by over 100 governments. Africa is central to achieving 30x30 and O'Donnell has been the primary political organiser behind getting African nations to formally commit. The 30x30 target now frames most major biodiversity finance conversations and sits behind the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework.

notable_work.description: Led Campaign for Nature advocacy that secured the 30x30 target in the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework at COP15, signed by 196 countries

profiles.name: Howard Buffett
profiles.summary: Chairman and CEO of the Howard G. Buffett Foundation and one of the most personally engaged conservation philanthropists working in Africa. Has committed hundreds of millions to anti-poaching, wildlife protection, and community conservation across Southern and East Africa including major programmes in DRC, South Africa, and Kenya. Son of Warren Buffett. His foundation is unusual for the scale of its field operations and the hands-on personal involvement of its principal in Africa conservation.

profiles.name: Makhtar Diop
profiles.summary: Managing Director of IFC and the most senior development finance official directly responsible for mobilising private capital into African markets. Senegalese. Under Diop IFC has expanded its nature and biodiversity finance portfolio significantly and is a co-financier on multiple African conservation and climate transactions.

profiles.name: Akinwumi Adesina
profiles.summary: President of the African Development Bank and the continent most senior multilateral development finance principal. Nigerian. Has championed the Desert to Power initiative and the African Development Fund climate windows, and positioned AfDB as a lead co-financier of nature-based investment plans across East, West, and Central Africa.

profiles.name: Samaila Zubairu
profiles.summary: President and CEO of the Africa Finance Corporation, a pan-African infrastructure and investment institution with a growing natural capital portfolio. AFC has been expanding its mandate into nature-based solutions and carbon markets as part of its climate finance strategy. Nigerian.

profiles.name: Nick O'Donohoe
profiles.role: Senior Fellow, Mossavar-Rahmani Center for Business and Government
profiles.summary: One of the architects of the impact investing field. Led British International Investment (BII), the UK development finance institution, as CEO from 2017 to November 2024 - during which the organisation committed more than £9 billion in new investments and deployed more than 60 percent of its portfolio into African markets. Previously co-founded Big Society Capital with Sir Ronald Cohen (2011-2015), and as Global Head of Research at JP Morgan co-authored the 2010 paper "Impact Investments: An Emerging Asset Class" with the Rockefeller Foundation, widely credited with coining the term. Started career at Goldman Sachs. MBA from Wharton. BA from Trinity College Dublin. Awarded CMG in 2022. Now a Senior Fellow at Harvard Kennedy School focused on development finance.

notable_work.description: Co-authored the 2010 JP Morgan and Rockefeller Foundation paper Impact Investments: An Emerging Asset Class, the first mainstream research to define and size the impact investing market - it projected a market opportunity of $400bn to $1trn
Led BII as CEO from 2017 to November 2024, committing more than £9 billion across Africa and Asia with more than 60 percent of the portfolio deployed into African markets

profiles.name: Françoise Lombard
profiles.summary: CEO of Proparco, the French development finance institution and subsidiary of AFD, appointed March 2022. Proparco is one of the most active bilateral DFIs in Francophone Africa and invests in sustainable agriculture, forestry, and conservation-linked private sector transactions across West and Central Africa. Began her career at the French Ministry of Defence. Has positioned Proparco as an active convener on climate finance for emerging markets - in 2025 launched the inaugural Proparco Days forum to connect DFI clients from the Global South on climate finance practice and green SME lending.

notable_work.description: Launched Proparco Days in 2025, a new climate finance forum connecting Proparco clients across the Global South on green financing, SME lending, and best practices in decarbonisation

profiles.name: Linda Broekhuizen
profiles.summary: Spent more than 20 years at FMO, the Dutch development bank, including eight years on the Management Board - first as Chief Investment Officer from 2014, then as interim CEO from 2020 until Michael Jongeneel took over in 2021. At FMO she founded the Agribusiness, Food and Water team and was a central figure in developing FMO sustainability and ESG standards that became a benchmark in impact investing. Since leaving FMO she chairs the board of Foodvalley NL, is vice-chair of the supervisory board of the Royal Tropical Institute (KIT), and is a member of the Development Cooperation Committee of the Dutch Advisory Council on International Affairs. Holds masters degrees in International Relations and Business Administration.

notable_work.description: Founded FMO Agribusiness, Food and Water team and shaped the institution ESG and sustainability approach over eight years on the Management Board, during which FMO grew to one of Europe largest bilateral development banks

profiles.name: Vera Songwe
profiles.summary: Chair of the African Climate Foundation and former Executive Secretary of the UN Economic Commission for Africa. Cameroonian. One of Africa most prominent architects of climate finance policy and the Bridgetown Initiative advocate. A leading voice on debt-for-nature swaps, SDR rechannelling, and the design of multilateral financial architecture that works for African nations.

profiles.name: Eelco Hoekstra
profiles.summary: CEO of Silvania, the global natural capital investment platform launched by commodity trader Mercuria with an initial $500 million commitment, appointed June 2025. Silvania invests in nature-based solutions globally - jurisdictional REDD+, reforestation, and ecosystem restoration - and is a co-founder of the Race to Belem campaign to mobilise private finance for Brazilian rainforest protection ahead of COP30. Hoekstra brings more than three decades in energy and infrastructure: he was CEO and Chairman of the Executive Board of Royal Vopak (2011-2022), the worlds largest independent tank storage company listed on Euronext Amsterdam, then joined the Executive Board of SHV Holding. Holds a masters in Economics from Erasmus University Rotterdam.

notable_work.description: Appointed CEO of Silvania in June 2025 to lead the Mercuria-backed $500M natural capital platform, which has since partnered with Conservation International and signed JREDD+ agreements with the Brazilian states of Tocantins, Piaui and Maranhao

profiles.name: Kate Hampton
profiles.summary: Chief Executive of CIFF, one of the largest climate-focused philanthropies in the world. CIFF has funded major African forest and land use programmes and is a significant funder of carbon market integrity work and biodiversity policy. Hampton has positioned CIFF as a catalytic funder that uses philanthropic capital to crowd in private investment.

profiles.name: Euan Murray
profiles.summary: CEO of TNFD, the Taskforce on Nature-related Financial Disclosures, which has developed the global framework for how companies and financial institutions report on nature-related risks and opportunities. TNFD adoption by major asset managers is reshaping how African nature assets are valued on institutional balance sheets and creating disclosure pressure that drives biodiversity finance demand.

profiles.name: Mark Gough
profiles.summary: CEO of the Capitals Coalition, which promotes natural, social, and human capital accounting as a standard for business decision-making. The Coalition Natural Capital Protocol is the framework used by companies seeking to quantify their dependencies and impacts on nature - directly relevant to how African biodiversity assets are valued in corporate supply chains and investment portfolios.

profiles.name: Nathan Fabian
profiles.summary: Chief Sustainable Systems Officer at the Principles for Responsible Investment (PRI), one of the largest investor networks in the world with more than 5,000 signatories. Also Secretary of the Taskforce on Net Zero Policy since June 2024. Previously served as chair of the European Platform on Sustainable Finance and as Rapporteur for the Technical Expert Group that framed the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities - one of the most consequential sustainable finance regulatory frameworks globally. Before PRI, was CEO of the Investor Group on Climate Change in Australia. Continues to speak on ESG integration, taxonomy design, and the role of investor coalitions in policy engagement.

notable_work.description: Served as Rapporteur for the EU Technical Expert Group on Sustainable Finance and chair of the European Platform on Sustainable Finance, shaping the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities - the primary EU framework for classifying green economic activity

profiles.name: Justine Leigh-Bell
profiles.summary: Executive Director of the Anthropocene Fixed Income Institute (AFII), a London and Stockholm based non-profit working to mobilise fixed income markets for the climate transition. Previously Deputy CEO and Director of Market Development at the Climate Bonds Initiative, where she spent eight years as the green bond market grew from $3 billion to over $1 trillion globally. Worked with governments and financial institutions across Europe, Africa, India, Southeast Asia and Latin America on national green bond policy and investment roadmaps. MSc Environmental Technology from Imperial College London and postgraduate training in molecular genomics at Yale.

notable_work.description: Held senior role at Climate Bonds Initiative across the period when the global green bond market grew from $3 billion to over $1 trillion, developing the Climate Bonds Taxonomy and supporting governments in emerging economies to access green capital markets

profiles.name: Dirk Forrister
profiles.summary: President and CEO of IETA, the principal trade association for carbon markets globally. IETA represents companies active in compliance and voluntary carbon markets and is a primary lobbying and standard-setting voice in Article 6 negotiations. Its positions on ITMO transfer rules and corresponding adjustments directly shape what African Article 6.2 deals look like.

profiles.name: Mark Kenber
profiles.summary: CEO of VCMI, which sets the rules for how companies can credibly claim they are using carbon credits as part of net-zero strategies. VCMI Claims Code of Practice determines which African carbon credits institutional buyers can use in their net-zero disclosures - directly influencing demand for African supply and the price premium available to high-integrity African projects.

profiles.name: Mikkel Larsen
profiles.summary: CEO of Climate Impact X, the Singapore-based voluntary carbon exchange backed by DBS Bank, Singapore Exchange, Standard Chartered, and Temasek. CIX operates spot and auction markets for high-quality carbon credits and has listed African REDD+ and ARR credits in its auctions. Its pricing benchmarks for nature-based credits are increasingly used as reference rates in bilateral African carbon transactions.

profiles.name: Steve Zwick
profiles.summary: Managing Editor of Ecosystem Marketplace, the primary news and data platform covering voluntary carbon and ecosystem service markets. Zwick has covered African carbon market development for over fifteen years and his reporting on credit quality controversies, pricing trends, and emerging African supply has directly shaped institutional understanding of what African credits are worth and why.

profiles.name: Gonzalo Munoz
profiles.summary: UN High-Level Climate Champion, leading the Race to Zero and Race to Resilience campaigns that mobilise corporate and financial sector commitments to net-zero. Chilean. The Champions office convenes the buyers and standard-setters that determine what voluntary carbon market demand looks like - and African supply is central to the nature-based solutions pipeline that underpins those commitments.

profiles.name: Peter Fearnhead
profiles.summary: CEO of African Parks, the organisation that manages 22 protected areas across 13 African countries under long-term delegated management agreements with national governments - the largest and most ecologically diverse portfolio under any single conservation organisation on the continent. African Parks operates on a business model that blends philanthropic anchor capital with tourism revenue and ecosystem service payments, and is the lead delivery partner in the Africa Keystone Protected Area Partnership. Fearnhead has built the model from its founding years under Dutch philanthropist Paul Fentener van Vlissingen and overseen its expansion to over 20 million hectares. RWF made a transformational $100 million five-year commitment to African Parks in 2021, the largest endowment gift in the organisation history.

notable_work.description: Led African Parks to a portfolio of 22 protected areas across 13 countries covering over 20 million hectares, with a mandate to manage 30 parks by 2030
Oversaw the Keystone Protected Area Partnership launch at UNGA September 2025, with African Parks as a founding partner alongside RWF, WCS, and Frankfurt Zoological Society

profiles.name: Andrew Muir
profiles.summary: CEO of Wilderness Foundation Africa and Wilderness Foundation Global, based in Port Elizabeth. Has led Wilderness Foundation Africa for over 37 years, building it into one of South Africa most respected conservation and social development NGOs and raising more than ZAR 1 billion for conservation and related causes. Was mentored by conservation icon Dr Ian Player and took over leadership of the organizations he founded, including the Wilderness Leadership School. Founded Umzi Wethu in 2006, an award-winning programme training vulnerable youth for employment. International Rolex Awards Laureate 2008, South African Conservationist of the Year 2007, Schwab Foundation Social Entrepreneur of the Year 2012. Masters in Environment and Development from University of Natal, honorary doctorate from University of KwaZulu-Natal.

notable_work.description: Founded Umzi Wethu programme in 2006, now an award-winning youth development initiative that has trained hundreds of vulnerable young South Africans for employment in the hospitality and conservation sectors

profiles.name: Jennifer Morris
profiles.summary: CEO of The Nature Conservancy, the world largest conservation NGO by budget. TNC has a significant Africa programme including field offices in multiple countries, the NatureVest investment arm, and involvement in multiple debt-for-nature negotiations currently underway across the continent. Morris oversees an organisation with annual revenues exceeding one billion dollars and direct relationships with the treasuries and environment ministries of African nations.

profiles.name: M. Sanjayan
profiles.summary: Former CEO of Conservation International, where he led the organisation for eight years. Sanjayan stepped down in August 2025 and was succeeded as interim CEO by Dr Daniela Raik. Under his tenure CI expanded its Africa programmes significantly across the Congo Basin, East Africa, and the Indian Ocean. A prominent communicator and policy voice on nature finance, Sanjayan remains active in the conservation space.

profiles.name: Carter Roberts
profiles.summary: President and CEO of WWF-US, the largest single affiliate in the WWF network. WWF-US is a significant funder and technical partner for African conservation programmes and has been instrumental in structuring debt-for-nature transactions in Africa, including providing technical assistance on the TNC-led swaps currently in negotiation. Roberts is a senior voice in Washington conservation finance policy.

profiles.name: Elizabeth Maruma Mrema
profiles.summary: Executive Secretary of the Convention on Biological Diversity, the treaty body that produced the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework - the 30x30 agreement now driving biodiversity finance globally. Tanzanian. The CBD sets the policy architecture that determines what counts as a biodiversity outcome, which in turn determines what biodiversity credits and conservation finance instruments are credible.

notable_work.description: Oversaw the adoption of the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework at COP15, establishing the 30x30 target that now frames global biodiversity finance

profiles.name: Inger Andersen
profiles.summary: Executive Director of UNEP, headquartered in Nairobi. Danish. UNEP is the lead UN agency for environmental policy and hosts the Finance Initiative that has produced some of the most influential guidance for banks and investors on nature-related risk. Its Nairobi base gives it particular engagement with African environmental governance and it is a co-sponsor of the biodiversity finance architecture underlying the GBF.

profiles.name: Simon Stiell
profiles.summary: Executive Secretary of the UNFCCC and the most senior official in the UN climate treaty architecture. Grenadian. His priorities and public positions on Article 6.2 operationalisation, corresponding adjustments, and the pace of bilateral deal-making directly determine the regulatory environment for every African ITMO transaction. Attended COP29 in Baku where Article 6.2 operational rules were finalised.

profiles.name: Rachel Kyte
profiles.summary: Dean of the Fletcher School at Tufts and former Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General for Sustainable Energy for All. British. One of the most connected climate finance diplomats in the world with deep relationships across development finance institutions, multilaterals, and African governments. Frequently called upon to advise on blended finance architecture and is an influential voice in the rooms where African climate finance frameworks are designed.

profiles.name: James Mwangi
profiles.summary: Group CEO of Equity Group, East Africa largest bank by customer numbers with operations across six African countries. Mwangi has positioned Equity as an active participant in green finance and has spoken publicly about the bank role in financing the transition to a low-carbon economy in East Africa. A rare African commercial bank leader engaged with conservation finance as a business opportunity rather than a compliance obligation.

profiles.name: Dambisa Moyo
profiles.summary: Zambian economist, author, and independent board director at major global corporations including 3M and Chevron. Author of Dead Aid, the most influential critique of development aid to Africa, and a prominent voice on how commercial capital rather than philanthropy should drive African development. Her framing of aid dependency as a structural problem for Africa is directly relevant to the shift from grant-funded to commercially self-sustaining conservation.

profiles.name: Andrew Deutz
profiles.role: Managing Director, Global Policy and Partnerships
profiles.summary: Managing Director for Global Policy and Partnerships at WWF, driving engagement with governments, international institutions, multilateral development banks, and global policy processes. Previously at The Nature Conservancy (2006-2022) as Managing Director for Global Policy and Conservation Finance. Three decades in multilateral environmental diplomacy, including senior roles at IUCN and advisory roles with the US State Department (as acting Lead Forest Negotiator) and the World Bank. Doctorate in International Environmental Law from the Fletcher School of Law and Diplomacy at Tufts University. One of the most influential practitioners at the intersection of international policy and conservation finance.

notable_work.description: Led TNC global policy work that helped structure landmark debt-for-nature swap deals in Seychelles and Belize, which became models for the global blue bonds and debt conversion movement

profiles.name: Jane Turpie
profiles.summary: Professor of Environmental Economics at UCT and one of Southern Africa most respected practitioners of ecosystem service valuation. Her work on the economics of South African protected areas, biodiversity offset markets, and payment for ecosystem services is foundational to how conservation finance instruments are designed and priced in the Southern African context.

profiles.name: Sarah Colenbrander
profiles.summary: Research Director at ODI covering climate finance flows, with a focus on how climate capital reaches developing countries and particularly African nations. Her research on finance delivery gaps, debt sustainability, and the effectiveness of blended finance in Sub-Saharan Africa is cited in policy discussions at the G20, COP, and in bilateral negotiations on climate finance commitments.

profiles.name: Paul Simpson
profiles.summary: CEO of CDP, the global disclosure platform through which over 23,000 companies and 1,100 cities report environmental data to investors. CDP forests and water disclosure programmes are increasingly used by African corporates and supply chain participants to demonstrate sustainability credentials. The CDP data set is a primary tool for investors assessing deforestation and nature-related risk in African operations.

profiles.name: Ed Northen
profiles.summary: Head of Sustainability at Actis, the emerging markets private equity and infrastructure firm with a large Africa portfolio. Actis has been developing its approach to natural capital and carbon across its Africa investments and Northen leads the integration of nature-related risk and opportunity into deal structuring. Actis manages over 12 billion dollars in assets across Africa and Asia.
profiles._note: Cannot verify this person at Actis. Sustainability partner at Actis is Shami Nissan. This entry may be incorrect - verify before publishing.

profiles.name: Robin Chazdon
profiles.summary: Professor Emerita and one of the world leading authorities on tropical forest regeneration science. Her research on natural forest regrowth - showing that secondary forests regenerate faster and at lower cost than plantations - underpins some of the most important methodological debates in ARR carbon project design. Directly relevant to VM0047 interpretation and the science behind African reforestation carbon credits.

profiles.name: Grethel Aguilar
profiles.summary: Director General of IUCN, the global authority on the status of the natural world. Costa Rican. IUCN manages the Red List of Threatened Species and the Green List of Protected Areas - the two most authoritative data sources used in biodiversity credit methodology design and conservation outcome verification. Its Africa member network is a key stakeholder in how international conservation standards apply at continental scale.

profiles.name: Marco Lambertini
profiles.summary: Convener of the Nature Positive Initiative, a coalition of conservation organizations, businesses and finance coalitions working to mainstream nature-positive commitments. Previously Director General of WWF International (2014-2022) and then WWF Special Envoy (2023), during which he was a key figure in the negotiation of the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework at CBD COP15 in December 2022. Before WWF, was CEO of BirdLife International. Author of "Becoming Nature Positive: Transitioning to a Safe and Just Future" (2025, Earthscan Routledge). Sits on the China Council for International Cooperation on Environment and Development, the Unilever Sustainability Advisory Council, the Prince Albert II of Monaco Foundation board, and co-chairs the Nature Action 100 Science Council.

notable_work.description: Central advocate in securing the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework at CBD COP15 in December 2022, including the 30x30 target and the $200 billion annual biodiversity finance goal

profiles.name: Laurence Tubiana
profiles.summary: CEO of the European Climate Foundation and architect of the Paris Agreement - France chief climate negotiator at COP21. One of the most influential figures in global climate policy. ECF funds a significant portfolio of work on carbon markets, nature finance, and African climate architecture. Her networks span European governments, major foundations, and African negotiating teams.

notable_work.description: Served as France chief climate negotiator and was central architect of the Paris Agreement adopted at COP21

profiles.name: Andrew Mitchell
profiles.summary: Founder of Global Canopy, an Oxford-based organisation producing financial intelligence on deforestation and nature risk used by major investors and banks. The Forest 500 rankings and the Supply Change database produced by Global Canopy directly affect how capital allocators assess exposure to African forest risk and which companies are held accountable for deforestation in their supply chains.

profiles.name: Ian Craig
profiles.summary: Director of Conservation at Lewa Wildlife Conservancy in northern Kenya, one of Africa most successful community conservation models and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Lewa has pioneered the integration of rhino and elephant protection with community benefit-sharing and is developing carbon finance as a revenue complement. Craig has spent decades building the relationships with Maasai and Samburu communities that underpin Lewa land tenure security.

profiles.name: Nigel Topping
profiles.summary: Chair of the UK Climate Change Committee (CCC), the statutory body that advises UK and devolved governments on emissions targets and reports to Parliament on climate progress. Appointed in July 2025, he had been a CCC member since May 2023. Before that he served as UN High Level Climate Champion for COP26, where he launched the Race to Zero and Race to Resilience campaigns and co-founded the Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero with Mark Carney. Earlier career included five years as CEO of We Mean Business and Executive Director at CDP. Awarded CMG in 2022. Mathematician by training from St Johns College Cambridge.

notable_work.description: As UN High Level Champion for COP26, launched Race to Zero (now more than 11,000 signatories) and Race to Resilience, and co-founded the Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero with Mark Carney
Appointed Chair of the UK Climate Change Committee by the UK Government and devolved administrations in July 2025

profiles.name: Tom Hall
profiles.summary: Head of natural capital investment at Mirova, working alongside Gautier Queru and Magali Rousselot on the Mirova Sustainable Land Fund and related vehicles. Mirova is among the most active fund managers deploying capital into African land use and forestry and Hall is involved in transaction origination and portfolio management across the Africa programme.

profiles.name: Jason Clay
profiles.role: Senior Vice President, Markets and Food; Executive Director, Markets Institute
profiles.summary: Senior Vice President and Executive Director of the Markets Institute at WWF-US. Clay has spent more than three decades working at the intersection of commodity supply chains and conservation - building multi-stakeholder roundtables on soy, palm oil, cotton, sugarcane, and beef that produced some of the most widely used voluntary sustainability standards in agriculture. In 1988 he invented Rainforest Marketing, one of the first fair-trade ecolabels in the US. PhD in anthropology and international agriculture from Cornell, with earlier study at Harvard and the London School of Economics. Won the 2012 James Beard Award for work on global food sustainability and is National Geographic first Food and Sustainability Fellow.

notable_work.description: Invented Rainforest Marketing in 1988, one of the first fair-trade ecolabels in the US, and co-created Rainforest Crunch with Ben and Jerrys plus more than 200 products with combined retail sales of $100 million
Built WWF Market Transformation program from 2006, convening producers, buyers, investors and NGOs to reduce the social and environmental impacts of major agricultural commodities globally

profiles.name: Peter Lindsey
profiles.summary: Zimbabwean conservation biologist directing the Lion Recovery Fund, an initiative of the Wildlife Conservation Network. Based in Harare and active across the savannah regions of Africa. Research and practice focus on funding mechanisms for protected areas, wildlife-based land uses, and community-private sector partnerships. Trained at Oxford and the Mammal Research Institute, University of Pretoria. Named as proposed director of the WCN Keystone Fund in the Africa Keystone Protected Areas Partnership framework.

notable_work.description: Director of the Lion Recovery Fund, coordinating investment in lion conservation across the African continent through WCN

profiles.name: El Hadji Mbaye Diagne
profiles.summary: One of Africa's most influential figures in international carbon market governance. Leads Afrique-Energie-Environnement, a Senegal-based organisation focused on energy and environmental policy across the continent.
